REFERENCE: The reference is a hallucinated composite, mixing a real paper title by different authors (Collins et al., 2020) with an incorrect name, date, and a fraudulent DOI.
authors: Akin Abayomi or Xiao-Jun Zhou are associated with similar fields, but Alakija is not the lead author of any BMJ paper with this title.
title: The title appears to be a modification of 'Machine learning and artificial intelligence research for patient benefit: 20 critical questions on transparency, replicability, ethics, and effectiveness' which is actually a real paper by Collins et al.
journal_name: BMJ (Clinical research ed.) is a valid and reputable medical journal.
journal_volume: Volume 368 corresponds to the year 2020, not 2018.
journal_issue: The citation provides pages and an elocator but lacks a numeric issue number, and journals in volume 368 use elocators starting with 'm'.
journal_page: The BMJ uses 'm' prefixed elocators (e.g., m151) rather than traditional page ranges like 562-578 for its primary articles in this volume.
elocator: The elocator e333760 does not match the BMJ's numbering format and does not exist for this volume.
publication_date: The provided date May 12, 2018, does not align with Volume 368 (2020) or the referenced DOI (2021).
doi: The DOI prefix 10.1126 belongs to Science Magazine, not the BMJ, and the suffix refers to a completely different Lancet article.
pmcid: No PMCID was provided in the citation string.
pmid: No PMID was provided in the citation string.

REFERENCE: All bibliographic elements are internally inconsistent and none correspond to a real, retrievable record. The DOI is especially indicative of fabrication.
authors: No record of an author named Olatunbosun-Alakija, A. publishing the cited article in BMJ.
title: The exact title does not appear in BMJ's table of contents or PubMed for 2018.
journal_name: BMJ (Clinical research ed.) is a real journal, but the volume/issue/page combination does not match any known BMJ issue.
journal_volume: Volume 368 of BMJ does not correspond to a 2018 publication; volume numbers for that year are far lower.
journal_issue: No issue information is provided, and the volume number itself is inaccurate.
journal_page: Pages 562-578 in volume 368 are not associated with any BMJ article matching the title.
elocator: Article number e333760 does not appear in BMJ's electronic locator system for the given volume.
publication_date: May 12 2018 does not align with BMJ's publishing schedule for volume 368.
doi: The DOI prefix 10.1126 belongs to Science (AAAS), while the suffix S0140-6736... is characteristic of The Lancet; the combination is invalid and does not resolve to any known article.
pmcid: No PMCID was supplied, and a search for the title yields no PMCID.
pmid: No PMID was supplied, and a search for the title yields no PMID.
